message.content: <local-command-caveat>Caveat: The messages below were generated by the user while running local commands. DO NOT respond to these messages or otherwise consider them in your response unless the user explicitly asks you to.</local-command-caveat>
configure the monitor to be 144 hz, but this is the desktops monitor not the laptops

content.text: The desktop monitor is `HDMI-A-1` (line 50). I'll add the 144Hz refresh rate to it.

content.content: The file /home/jofre/.dotfiles/config/niri/config.kdl has been updated successfully.

content.text: Done. Set `HDMI-A-1` to `1920x1080@144.000`. Niri should hot-reload the config automatically.

message.content: my laptop uses the same monitor name, will it cause problems?

content.text:
No, it won't cause problems. Niri simply ignores output entries that don't match any connected monitor. On your laptop, there's no `HDMI-A-1` plugged in, so that block is skipped and only the `eDP-1` config applies.

If you do plug an external monitor into the laptop via HDMI, it would pick up this 144Hz setting — but that would only matter if that monitor doesn't support 144Hz, in which case niri would fall back to the closest available mode.

lastPrompt: my laptop uses the same monitor name, will it cause problems?
